summary: Rachel Torres and Chris Lee held a renewal call with Isaac Brennan of Trailhead, covering fourteen months of experience with Protect and Detect. Isaac acknowledged a mid-March six-hour outage as a concern but praised the thorough post-mortem and the subsequent technical fixes. The team discussed adding Aegis Comply v2 at a promotional rate of $18,500 annually (locked for two years), which Isaac found compelling given upcoming PCI audit cycles. Isaac indicated strong internal support for the renewal and agreed on next steps including a procurement one-pager, contract execution by end of April, and a technical onboarding call post-signing.
actionItems: Rachel Torres: Send Isaac a one-pager with business case and ROI framing around compliance time savings for CFO/procurement by end of week
Rachel Torres: Loop in contracts team to issue formal renewal documents with target signing before end of April
Chris Lee: Schedule and lead a technical onboarding call with Isaac's infrastructure team after contract is signed
Isaac Brennan: Run the renewal proposal through internal procurement and advocate for approval

keyMoments.text: Isaac raises the mid-March six-hour Detect outage that left his team blind during a promotional weekend
keyMoments.speaker: Isaac Brennan

keyMoments.text: Isaac confirms his team noticed improvements since the redundancy fixes and calls the platform 'rock solid', crediting follow-through for why they're renewing instead of shopping around
keyMoments.speaker: Isaac Brennan

keyMoments.text: Isaac reacts positively to Aegis Comply v2's on-demand PCI DSS reporting, noting his compliance team spends six weeks in spreadsheets for audits annually
keyMoments.speaker: Isaac Brennan

keyMoments.text: Rachel presents the promotional Comply add-on rate of $18,500 (vs. $22,000 standard) locked for a two-year term; Isaac signals no blockers and commits to advocating internally
keyMoments.speaker: Rachel Torres
